sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 17.103 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.17)

statement.latex: Does there exist a continuum of sets$$of primes for which every finite group possessing a Hall$$-subgroup is a$D_$-group? Comment of 2025: It has been proved that, for any integer$x$, if$$is the set of all prime numbers$p>x$, then any finite group containing a Hall$$-subgroup is a$D_$-group (K.\,A.\,Ilenko, N.\,V.\,Maslova, Siberian Math. J., 62, no.\,1 (2021), 44--51). { D.\,O.\,Revin}

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory IV_PROBLEM_UNDEFINED; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
